title: The `::` type annotation operator as four bold slab squares. In type theory, `x :: T` means 'x has type T' — a compile-time structural declaration that something exists as exactly that kind of thing. Precept's contract IS a type annotation for the entire domain: it declares what kinds of field values, state transitions, and event signatures are structurally possible.
conceptualBridge: The double-colon means Precept's contract is a type declaration because `::` IS the compile-time assertion of structural kind — not a runtime check, a definition of what can exist.
decision: Shortlisted: avg 4.31, no hard fails — the `::` type-annotation operator as four bold squares; the two-pairs-with-gap layout differentiates from a plain 2×2 grid; vivid red on dark is a fresh palette in the set; maximum visual economy score.
